  • Open

    McDonald's Introduces New Buffalo Ranch and New Chips Ahoy! Frappe
    McDonald's has released its new, limited-time Buffalo Ranch and new, limited-time Chips Ahoy! Frappe. While the official launch isn't until October 28, 2025, some locations are already serving the new sauce and drink. McDonald's Buffalo Ranch is available as a dipping sauce and is featured in the Bacon Buffalo Ranch McCrispy sandwich and Buffalo Ranch Snack Wrap. The Bacon Buffalo Ranch McCrispy is available in a regular and Deluxe version. Here's what's in each version of the sandwich: - Bacon Buffalo Ranch McCrispy - A McCrispy chicken filet, Buffalo Ranch, applewood-smoked bacon, and crinkle-cut pickles, served on a toasted potato roll. It costs $6.59 near me in Los Angeles. - Bacon Buffalo Ranch Deluxe McCrispy - A McCrispy chicken filet, Buffalo Ranch sauce, applewood-smoked bacon, lettuce, and tomato, served on a toasted potato roll. The sandwich is priced at $7.19 near me. The Buffalo Ranch Snack Wrap features a McCrispy Strip, Buffalo Ranch sauce, shredded cheese, and shredded lettuce, wrapped in a soft flour tortilla (i.e., it's the Ranch Snack Wrap, but with Buffalo Ranch sauce instead of Ranch). It's priced at $2.99. If the trio seems familiar, it's the same menu as for the outgoing Special Edition Gold Sauce, only with Buffalo Ranch. You can also get a dipping cup of the new sauce with an order of McCrispy Strips or Chicken McNuggets. You can also purchase the sauce cup separately (prices vary, but it costs $0.29 near me). The new Chips Ahoy! Frappe is a coffee frappe blended with chocolate chip cookie syrup and Chips Ahoy! cookie pieces, topped with whipped cream and more Chips Ahoy! cookie pieces. Prices vary, but a small Chips Ahoy! Frappe costs $3.39 here in Los Angeles. Photo via McDonald's. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )

  • Open

    Taco Bell Tests New Gordita Crunch Sliders
    Taco Bell is testing new Gordita Crunch Sliders for a limited time at participating locations in the Nashville area. The new menu item features a set of two snack-sized pillowy flatbreads folded around crispy white-corn shells with a layer of melted three-cheese blend to hold them together. Nestled inside the shells is a filling of your choice of seasoned beef, slow-roasted chicken, or grilled, marinated steak, plus Creamy Chipotle sauce and pico de gallo. They range from $4.49 to $5.49 (varying with location) for the chicken or steak versions and $3.49 for the seasoned beef version. While essentially a mini rendition of the Cheesy Gordita Crunch, the Gordita Crunch Sliders sport a slightly different taco shell (made of white corn instead of yellow corn) and a different combination of fillings (the Cheesy Gordita Crunch is filled with seasoned beef, shredded lettuce, shredded cheddar cheese, and Spicy Ranch). Gordita Crunch Sliders are also available as part of a $9 Discovery Luxe Cravings Box and a $6.99 Snack Trio, which is an in-app-only item available at select locations. The $9 Discovery Luxe Cravings Box includes a set of two Gordita Crunch Sliders, a Nacho Cheese Doritos Locos Taco, a Crunchy Taco, Cinnamon Twists, and a medium fountain drink. The $6.99 Snack Trio includes a set of two Gordita Crunch Sliders, Nacho Fries with nacho cheese sauce, and two Cinnabon Delights. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )
    New Cookie Butter Chips Ahoy! Cookies Available Now
    Nabisco launches new Cookie Butter-inspired Chewy Chips Ahoy! cookies ahead of the 2025 holiday season. The limited-edition flavor features the brand's signature soft, chewy cookie with white fudge chips and a creamy cookie butter-flavored filling. You can find Cookie Butter-inspired Chewy Chips Ahoy! now at retailers nationwide for a limited time. They come in a 9.9-oz package. Prices vary, but my local Walmart here in Los Angeles sells them for $4.78. That's a premium price, considering a 13.3-oz package of regular Chewy Chips Ahoy costs $3.28 at the same store. However, it's comparable in price to the limited-edition Stranger Things Chips Ahoy! cookies. Photo via Chips Ahoy!. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

    Checkers & Rally's Tests Two New Spicy Burgers
    Checkers & Rally's are testing two new spicy burgers, the Hatch Canyon Burger and Snake Bite Burger, for a limited time at select locations in Southern California and Arizona. The Hatch Canyon Burger (pictured) features a large beef patty, American cheese, hatch green chiles, crispy-fried onions, and Secret Sauce, served on a toasted, bakery-style bun. Prices vary, but the burger costs $4.99 at the nearest Rally's in Los Angeles. The Snake Bite Burger features a large beef patty, American cheese, sauteed jalapenos & onions, crispy-fried onions, and Secret Sauce, served on a toasted, bakery-style bun. If you're following along, the only difference between the two spicy burgers is that one has hatch green chiles, while the other contains sauteed jalapenos and onions. Like the Hatch Canyon Burger, the Snake Bite Burger costs $4.99. Photo via Checkers & Rally's. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Sonic Offers Weekly "Spooky Savings" Deals Through November 2, 2025
    In celebration of Halloween, Sonic is offering a series of weekly "Spooky Savings" deals exclusively through the Sonic website or mobile app until Sunday, November 2, 2025. The schedule of "Spooky Savings" deals goes as follows: Through October 26, 2025: Free Medium Tots or Groovy Fries with purchase. Free Small Classic Shake with purchase. Free Sonic Cheeseburger with purchase. Free Small Premium Chicken Bites with purchase. Through November 2, 2025: Free Entree with purchase (not available on October 31, 2025) Free Medium Slush with purchase. Free Trick or Treat Blast with purchase. Free Small Snack or Side with purchase. In addition to the weekly deals, Sonic will again be selling corn dogs for 50 cents each on Halloween, Friday, October 31, 2025, at participating locations nationwide. Photo via Sonic. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

  • Open

    Jimmy John's Brings Back the Picklewich
    Jimmy John's brings back the Picklewich for a limited time nationwide on Monday, October 27, 2025, but its loyalty members get early access and can order it starting Friday, October 24, 2025. The Picklewich is just like the chain's regular sandwiches, only served in a split kosher dill pickle rather than on bread. In addition to the returning Vito Picklewich and Turkey Picklewich, Jimmy John's adds four new varieties: Ham, Roasted, Tuna, and Veggie. Also new this time is the debut of Pickle Ranch and the Triple Pickle meal. Pickle Jimmy Chips (pickle-flavored potato chips) are also back. The Triple Pickle includes a choice of Picklewich, a bag of Pickle Jimmy Chips, and Pickle Ranch. Photo via Jimmy John's. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Ghirardelli Releases New Dubai Style Chocolates
    The Ghirardelli Chocolate Company joins the Dubai chocolate trend with the launch of new Ghirardelli Dubai Style Chocolate, which are an individually-wrapped, bite-sized take on the popular trend. Arriving in time for the holiday season, the new chocolate features a milk chocolate shell surrounding a core of pistachio filling layered with finely shredded kataifi. Ghirardelli Dubai Style Chocolates are available now on the Ghirardelli website and are rolling out to Ghirardelli Chocolate Shops. They will also be available at select retailers starting in November. The new chocolates are available in a 4-oz bag for $13.95 as well as large gift and bundle sizes. A 50-count gift box for $75 and a case pack (260 pieces) for $299.95 are available on the Ghirardelli website. Photo via Ghirardelli. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Jack-O'-Lantern Pizza is Back at Papa John's for Halloween 2025
    The pumpkin-shaped Jack-O-Lantern Pizza makes its annual return to the Papa Johns menu for the 2025 Halloween season. The seasonal pizza features a thin-crust pizza in the shape of a pumpkin topped with cheese, pepperoni for eyes and a smiling mouth, and olives for pupils. It's served unsliced (to maintain its shape) with a seasoning packet and pepperoncini. The Jack-O-Lantern Pizza is available now for a limited time at participating Papa Johns locations nationwide. The price for the pizza varies (it starts at $11.99), but it costs $12.99 at my local Papa Johns here in Los Angeles. Photo via Papa Johns. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    The Gobbler Returns to Wawa
    The seasonal Gobbler is back at Wawa convenience stores for the 2025 fall season. The Thanksgiving-friendly sandwich features hot turkey in gravy (you can opt for cold sliced turkey [not in gravy]) that can be customized with several fixings, including cranberry sauce, stuffing, and mashed potatoes. New options this year include Double Stuffed Gobblers and Gobbler Wraps. Double Stuffed Gobblers are hot turkey sandwiches with either a double portion of stuffing or a combination of stuffing and either mashed potatoes (pictured) or mashed sweet potatoes. Like the regular Gobbler, you can select your roll, cheese, and spreads (cranberry is recommended). Gobbler Wraps feature the same fillings as a regular Gobbler sandwich, only rolled up inside a flour tortilla. In addition to a hot or cold sandwich or wrap, there's the  Gobbler Bowl, which offers a base of stuffing, mac & cheese, mashed potatoes, or sweet potatoes (or a pairing of two of the four options), topped with hot turkey and your choice of toppings, spreads, and seasonings. The Gobbler is available now at participating Wawa locations chain-wide for a limited time. Photo via Wawa. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

  • Open

    Sonic Introduces New Pretzel Bacon Smasher Burger and Peppermint Brownie Bark Shake
    It's not even Halloween yet, but Sonic is moving on to the holidays with the introduction of the new Pretzel Bacon Sonic Smasher burger and Peppermint Brownie Bark Shake, which are available now in the Sonic App ahead of a national launch starting November 3, 2025. The fast-food chain has also updated its value menu. The Pretzel Bacon Double Sonic Smasher features smashed and grilled Angus beef patties, American cheese, bacon, crispy-fried onions, and creamy mustard, served on a soft, buttery pretzel bun. A triple-patty version is also available for bigger appetites. Prices start at $7.39 for the Double and $8.39 for the Triple. The Peppermint Brownie Bark Shake features vanilla soft serve blended with chocolate chunk brownie pieces and crushed peppermints, topped with whipped topping, a maraschino cherry, and more crushed peppermints. The price for the shake starts at $5.39 for a medium. Both items are available for a limited time at participating Sonic restaurants nationwide. Sonic has revamped its $1.99 Menu with an updated lineup that includes the New Creamy Mustard Chicken Tender Wrap. The snack-sized wrap features a white meat, fried chicken tender wrapped in a soft, flour tortilla with lettuce, tomato, cheddar cheese, and creamy mustard sauce. Other items on the updated value menu include: Cheesy Baja Crispy Tender Wrap  Jr. Double Cheeseburger  Medium Cherry Flavor Bubble Slush  Medium Green Apple Flavor Bubble Slush  Salted Caramel Toffee Croissant Bites  Photo via Sonic Drive-In. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )
    Wienerschnitzel Blends New Hostess Shakes
    Wienerschnitzel launches new Hostess Shakes as its latest limited-time milkshake offering. The new menu items feature an entire snack cake blended into a shake. The new shakes are available in two varieties: the Hostess Ding Dongs Shake and the Hostess Twinkies Shake. The Hostess Ding Dongs Shake takes a Hostess Ding Dongs snack cake and blends it with vanilla Tastee Freeze soft serve. Similarly, the Hostess Twinkies Shake consists of a Twinkies snack cake blended with vanilla Tastee Freeze soft serve. Prices vary, but the Hostess Shakes cost $6.19 here in Los Angeles. Photo via Wienerschnitzel. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Taco Bell Digital Deals Through Mid-November 2025
    Taco Bell has released its digital deal schedule for the rest of October through mid-November 2025 (some deals go through December). As usual, the calendar includes a mix of Tuesday Drops (exclusive to Taco Bell Rewards members), in-app deals, and third-party delivery deals. Here's what the upcoming deals look like: Tuesday Drops 10/21/25 - "Decades Item Picklist" (you can pick any Decades menu item) for the first 30,000 users starting at 2PM PT (limited to one per user). 10/28/25 - Get a $1 Chalupa for one hour from 2PM to 3PM PT (limited to one per user). 11/4/25 - Get a $1 Cheesy Gordita Crunch for one hour from 2PM to 3PM PT (limited to one per user). In-App Deals $0 delivery fee when you purchase any Cantina Chicken menu item (excluding an Avocado Verde Salsa sauce packet) on…  ( 16 min )

  • Open

    Dunkin's 2025 Halloween Menu Arrives
    Dunkin's 2025 Halloween menu has arrived. The seasonal menu includes the launch of the new Candy Bar Signature Latte along with the return of the Spider Donut and the Halloween Munchkins Bucket. The new Candy Bar Signature Latte features espresso and your choice of milk mixed with a blend of chocolate, toffee, pretzel, and peanut butter flavors, topped with whipped cream, a mocha drizzle, and crumbled candy bar pieces. You can get the new beverage hot or iced. The Spider Donut is a donut with purple icing, topped with a Glazed Chocolate Munchkins donut hole and decorated with chocolate drizzle "spider legs" and vanilla-flavored icing "eyes." This year's Halloween Munchkins Bucket gets a new look with a purple Spider Donut-inspired lid on a black bucket featuring a Halloween motif that includes Spider Donut illustrations as well as depictions of Munchkins dressed in costume. The bucket comes filled with 50 Munchkins, including Halloween-themed Munchkins covered in chocolate-and-orange sprinkles (Dunkin's classic Chocolate, Vanilla, and Strawberry Frosted donuts also get the chocolate-and-orange sprinkles instead of their regular sprinkles for the Halloween season). Dunkin's 2025 Halloween menu is available now for a limited time at participating locations nationwide. Photo via Dunkin'. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Review: Taco Bell - Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito
    Taco Bell's Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito features Flamin' Hot Fritos, new Flamin' Hot Rice, seasoned beef, a three-cheese blend, Creamy Chipotle sauce, warm nacho cheese sauce, and reduced-fat sour cream inside a warm flour tortilla topped with grilled cheese. The burrito costs $6.49 at my local Taco Bell, but is also available as part of a $9 Discovery Luxe Cravings Box that includes the burrito, a Chicken Chalupa Supreme, a Crunchy Taco, Chips & Nacho Cheese Sauce, and a medium fountain drink, so I paid the extra $2.50 for a lot more food. According to Taco Bell, the Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito is meant to be "cheesier" and "spicier" than its predecessor, the Beefy Crunch Burrito. With the extra layer of melted cheese on the outside, it's definitely cheesier but I can't say it's any spicier. While the Flamin' Hot Rice gave the fillings a bold red hue, I picked out some grains and found the flavor to be surprisingly mild given the visuals. Mostly, it just added texture and filler. I took my paritcular burrito on a short drive home to eat it and the Fritos were throughly softened. They provided a bulk of the savory and spicy Flamin' Hot flavor as well as a hit of fried corn. The sauces (Creamy Chipotle, nacho cheese, and sour cream) were mostly lost amid the Flamin' Hot, seasoned beef, and grilled cheese flavors. Overall, I liked Taco Bell's Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito a fair bit. The Flamin' Hot flavor goes well with the generally beefy cheesiness and there was a decent variety of textures. However, if you consider that they tested a $2 version of almost the same burrito earlier this year, minus the grilled cheese (the price is in line with the regular Grilled Cheese Burrito, however), the Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito is quite pricey and makes getting the $9 combo a no-brainer. Nutritional Info - Taco Bell Flamin' Hot Grilled Cheese Burrito Calories - 730 Fat - 41g (Saturated Fat - 17g) Sodium - 1270mg Carbs - 65g (Sugar - 4g) Protein - 26g Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 16 min )

  • Open

    Crumbl Brings Back Dubai Chocolate Brownie Through October 25, 2025
    Crumbl brings back its Dubai Chocolate Brownie as part of this week's limited-time dessert lineup available from October 20 through October 25, 2025, at locations nationwide. Here's what this week's Crumbl dessert menu looks like: - Dubai Chocolate Brownie - A chilled brownie with a crunchy kataifi and pistachio cream filling, topped with a layer of milk chocolate and a drizzle of pistachio cream. - Tres Leches Cake - A vanilla cake soaked in a classic tres leches sauce, topped with whipped cream and cinnamon. - Cookies & Cream Cookie - A warm marbled dark chocolate and vanilla cookie packed with white drops and topped with a white drizzle and cookies & cream pieces. - Cereal Milk Cookie - A warm cereal-inspired sugar cookie brushed with a milky glaze and topped with fruity cereal. - Original M&M's Cookie - A warm cookie packed with Milk Chocolate M&M's candies. - Banana Bread Cookie - A warm banana cookie infused with brown sugar, cinnamon, and vanilla, topped with a cream cheese glaze and a brown sugar streusel. - Milk Chocolate Chip Cookie - A warm cookie packed with milk chocolate chips. - Weekly "Secret Menu" - A mystery cookie or dessert that varies with location. You can check the Crumbl website or mobile app to see what's available near you. Photo via Crumbl. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
    Big Box Deal Returns to Popeyes
    The Big Box combo meal is back at Popeyes for a limited time this fall. Popeyes' Big Box includes two pieces of fried chicken or three chicken tenders plus two regular sides and a biscuit. Prices vary, but it costs $6.99 here in Los Angeles (it's $6 in some areas). Popeyes is also offering a $1 wings deal throughout October. You can get either 12 wings for $12 or 18 wings for $18. Photo via Popeyes. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

  • Open

    Taco Bell Tests New Cantina Fajita Menu
    Taco Bell is considering a potential expansion of its Cantina Chicken Menu with its new Cantina Fajita test menu at participating locations in the Dallas, TX area. The key feature of the menu is seasoned bell peppers and onions paired with either carne asada (steak) or slow-roasted chicken. As with the Cantina Menu, all items come with a packet of Avocado Verde Salsa. The Cantina Fajita menu includes: - Cantina Fajita Bowl - A bed of seasoned rice topped with simmered black beans, pico de gallo, guacamole, reduced-fat sour cream, seasoned bell peppers and onions, a three-cheese blend, Creamy Jalapeno sauce, and a double portion of carne asada or slow-roasted chicken. Two small warm flour tortillas are included on the side. It's priced at $7.99 with either protein option. - Cantina Fajita Grilled Cheese Burrito - Seasoned rice, carne asada or slow-roasted chicken, seasoned bell peppers and onions, Creamy Jalapeno sauce, and a three-cheese blend, wrapped in a warm flour tortilla and topped with a layer of grilled cheese. The suggested price for the burrito is $5.99. - Cantina Fajita Grilled Soft Taco - A soft flour tortilla folded and grilled with carne asada or slow-roasted chicken, seasoned bell peppers and onions, Creamy Jalapeno sauce, and a three-cheese blend. It's priced at $3.49. - Cantina Fajita Quesadilla - A large flour tortilla folded with carne asada or slow-roasted chicken, seasoned bell peppers and onions, Creamy Jalapeno sauce, and a three-cheese blend, and then grilled. It costs $6.49. - Cantina Fajita Discovery Luxe Box - For $9, the box includes a Cantina Fajita Grilled Soft Taco (carne asada or slow-roasted chicken), Doritos Locos Taco, Beefy 5-Layer Burrito, Cinnamon Twists, and a medium fountain drink. Photo via Taco Bell. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )

    Popeyes' Cajun-Style Turkey Returns for 2025 Thanksgiving Season
    Popeyes' Cajun-Style Turkey makes its annual return for the 2025 Thanksgiving season. Available for a limited time at participating locations as well as online for nationwide (continental US only) shipping to your door, the pre-seasoned, fully-cooked turkey needs to be thawed and reheated before eating. Each Cajun-Style Turkey weighs about 11 to 13 pounds and is meant to serve eight to 12 people. You can pre-order it in person or through the Popeyes website or mobile app. Pricing for the Cajun turkey starts at $54.99 when purchased from a Popeyes restaurant and $99.99 to get one shipped to your home. If you're curious as to how Popeyes' Cajun-Style Turkey turns out, I tried and reviewed one a while back. Photo via Popeyes. Read more at Brand Eating!  ( 15 min )
2025-10-26T16:19:57.785Z osmosfeed 1.15.1
